content.text: {"overview":"This A-major fugue in 9/8 unfolds with rigorous contrapuntal architecture across five clearly delineated sections, tracing a complete circle-of-fifths journey through E major, f# minor, D major, b minor, and e minor before returning emphatically to the tonic. Its most distinctive feature is the stretto-driven development section where subject entries in close imitation intensify the harmonic tension through minor-mode regions, culminating in a virtuosic sequential episode that drives the resolution back to A major.","cross_piece_tags":["fugue","counterpoint","circle_of_fifths","dramatic","modal_mixture"]}
